summary:
## Security Analysis: XStream 1.4.15 (`com.thoughtworks.xstream:xstream`)

### Overview
XStream is a Java XML serialization library. Version 1.4.15 contains multiple critical security vulnerabilities across its deserialization pipeline, primarily related to **unsafe deserialization (CWE-502)** and **externally-controlled class loading (CWE-470)**. The library's architecture of converting XML element/attribute values directly into Java class names and instantiating them is the root cause of a large family of CVEs (CVE-2021-21341 through CVE-2021-21351 and beyond).

---

### Critical Findings

#### 1. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `DefaultMapper.realClass()` loads arbitrary classes from XML
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/mapper/DefaultMapper.java` (line 81, 149)

`DefaultMapper.realClass()` calls `Class.forName()` with a class name taken directly from the XML `class` attribute. This is the core class-loading entry point for all deserialization. Any XML element with a `class="..."` attribute controls which Java class is instantiated. Without a strict type allowlist, this enables arbitrary object instantiation.

---

#### 2. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `AbstractReflectionConverter.doUnmarshal()` — multiple `realClass()` and `newInstance()` calls
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/reflection/AbstractReflectionConverter.java` (lines 342, 350, 406, 426, 531, 555, 565, 567)

`AbstractReflectionConverter.doUnmarshal()` calls `realClass()` to resolve types from XML and `newInstance()` to instantiate them. This converter handles most Java objects during deserialization and directly controls which classes are instantiated based on XML content.

---

#### 3. CWE-502 — `DynamicProxyConverter` creates `java.lang.reflect.Proxy` from XML-controlled interface/handler names
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/DynamicProxyConverter.java` (lines 124, 131)

`DynamicProxyConverter.unmarshal()` calls `Proxy.newProxyInstance()` with an `InvocationHandler` and interface types loaded from XML. An attacker can specify a malicious `InvocationHandler` class (e.g., `java.beans.EventHandler`) and arbitrary interfaces, constructing proxy-based gadget chains that trigger RCE on method invocation. This is the basis of multiple CVEs (CVE-2021-21346, CVE-2021-21350).

Also found in `CGLIBEnhancedConverter.java` (line 344).

---

#### 4. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal()` creates CGLIB proxies from XML-controlled classes
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/reflection/CGLIBEnhancedConverter.java` (lines 352, 358, 361, 397, 403)

`CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal()` calls `setSuperclass()`, `setInterfaces()`, `realClass()`, and `create()` with class names from XML. This enables attackers to create CGLIB-enhanced proxies from any class, enabling gadget chain execution via callback mechanisms (CVE-2021-21344, CVE-2021-21345).

---

#### 5. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `JavaClassConverter.fromString()` converts XML text to arbitrary `Class` objects
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/JavaClassConverter.java` (line 76)

`JavaClassConverter.fromString()` calls `realClass()` (which uses `Class.forName()`) with the XML text content as the class name. Any `<java.lang.Class>` element in deserialized XML can reference any class on the classpath, enabling type confusion and gadget chain assembly.

---

#### 6. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `JavaMethodConverter.unmarshal()` reconstructs arbitrary `Method`/`Constructor` objects
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/JavaMethodConverter.java` (lines 141, 143)

`JavaMethodConverter.unmarshal()` calls `getDeclaredMethod()` and `getDeclaredConstructor()` with class and method names from XML. This reconstructs arbitrary `java.lang.reflect.Method` or `Constructor` objects, which can then be used as part of reflection-based gadget chains.

---

#### 7. CWE-502 — `ExternalizableConverter.unmarshal()` calls `readExternal()` on XML-controlled objects
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/reflection/ExternalizableConverter.java` (line 192)

`ExternalizableConverter` instantiates objects of XML-controlled types and calls `readExternal()` on them. Since `readExternal()` implementations can contain arbitrary logic (file I/O, JNDI lookups, etc.), this enables code execution on any `Externalizable` class present on the classpath.

---

#### 8. CWE-502 / CWE-094 — `SerializationMembers` reflectively invokes `readResolve`, `readObject`, `writeReplace`
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/core/util/SerializationMembers.java` (lines 74, 97, 126, 146)

`SerializationMembers` uses `Method.invoke()` to call `readResolve()`, `writeReplace()`, `readObject()`, and `writeObject()` on deserialized objects. These are the canonical gadget chain trigger points — classes like `java.util.PriorityQueue`, `org.apache.commons.collections` transformers, and others execute dangerous logic in these callbacks.

---

#### 9. CWE-502 — `XStreamer.fromXML()` and `PureJavaReflectionProvider.instantiateUsingSerialization()` call `ObjectInputStream.readObject()`
**Files:**
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/XStreamer.java` (lines 271, 275)
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/reflection/PureJavaReflectionProvider.java` (line 136)
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/core/util/CustomObjectInputStream.java` (line 144)

XStream internally calls `ObjectInputStream.readObject()` and `readUnshared()` for objects that implement `Serializable`. This creates a second-order deserialization path where the Java serialization gadget chains (Commons Collections, Spring, etc.) are triggered within XStream deserialization.

Also noted in `AnnotationMapper.readObject()` (line 521) and `PackageAliasingMapper.readObject()` (line 100).

---

#### 10. CWE-502 — `SubjectConverter.unmarshal()` deserializes `Principal`/`Subject` with XML-controlled class names
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/SubjectConverter.java` (lines 84–87)

`SubjectConverter.unmarshal()` calls `unmarshalPrincipals()`, `unmarshalPublicCredentials()`, `unmarshalPrivateCredentials()`, and `unmarshalReadOnly()` which all load and instantiate class types from XML content. This is the basis of CVE-2021-21347.

---

#### 11. CWE-502 — `SerializableConverter.doUnmarshal()` resolves superclass types from XML
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/reflection/SerializableConverter.java` (lines 462, 464)

`SerializableConverter.doUnmarshal()` calls `realClass()` with serialized class hierarchy information from XML, enabling type confusion attacks when deserializing `Serializable` objects.

---

#### 12. CWE-470 — `EnumConverter`, `EnumSetConverter`, `EnumMapConverter` load enum classes from XML
**Files:**
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/enums/EnumConverter.java` (line 55)
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/enums/EnumMapConverter.java` (line 65)
- `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/enums/EnumSetConverter.java` (line 83)

Enum converters call `realClass()` or `valueOf()` with enum type names from XML. While limited to enums in intent, the class-loading mechanism can be abused in the broader deserialization attack surface.

---

#### 13. CWE-502 / CWE-470 — `RecordConverter` uses `Class.forName()` and `Method.invoke()` for Java Records
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/RecordConverter.java` (lines 58, 60, 203, 222)

`RecordConverter.classForName()` and `RecordConverter.componentValue()` use `Class.forName()` and `Method.invoke()` with names from XML content. This adds an additional attack surface for Java Record type deserialization.

---

#### 14. CWE-470 — `XStream.registerConverterDynamically()` uses `Class.forName()` for converter registration
**File:** `xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/XStream.java` (line 1088)

`XStream.registerConverterDynamically()` calls `Class.forName()` to load converter classes by name. If this is called with user-controlled input, arbitrary classes can be loaded and instantiated as converters.

---

### Summary Table

| Severity | Finding | CWE | File(s) |
|----------|---------|-----|---------|
| Critical | AnyTypePermission configured | CWE-502 | XStream.java |
| Critical | DefaultMapper.realClass() — arbitrary class loading | CWE-470, CWE-502 | DefaultMapper.java |
| Critical | AbstractReflectionConverter — XML-controlled class instantiation | CWE-502, CWE-470 | AbstractReflectionConverter.java |
| Critical | DynamicProxyConverter — Proxy with XML-controlled handler/interfaces | CWE-502, CWE-470 | DynamicProxyConverter.java |
| Critical | CGLIBEnhancedConverter — CGLIB proxy from XML class names | CWE-502, CWE-470 | CGLIBEnhancedConverter.java |
| Critical | SerializationMembers — Method.invoke() on gadget callbacks | CWE-502, CWE-094 | SerializationMembers.java |
| Critical | ObjectInputStream.readObject() in XStream pipeline | CWE-502 | XStreamer.java, PureJavaReflectionProvider.java |
| Critical | ExternalizableConverter.readExternal() on arbitrary types | CWE-502 | ExternalizableConverter.java |
| Critical | SubjectConverter — Principal class instantiation from XML | CWE-502, CWE-470 | SubjectConverter.java |
| High | JavaClassConverter — XML text to Class object | CWE-470, CWE-502 | JavaClassConverter.java |
| High | JavaMethodConverter — arbitrary Method/Constructor from XML | CWE-502, CWE-470 | JavaMethodConverter.java |
| High | SerializableConverter — superclass hierarchy from XML | CWE-502, CWE-470 | SerializableConverter.java |
| High | EnumConverter/EnumSetConverter/EnumMapConverter — class loading | CWE-470, CWE-502 | Enum converters |
| High | RecordConverter — Class.forName() with XML names | CWE-502, CWE-470 | RecordConverter.java |

### Recommendation
XStream 1.4.15 is known-vulnerable. Users should upgrade to XStream ≥ 1.4.19 (which addresses the 2021 CVE batch) and configure explicit type allowlists via `XStream.allowTypes()` / `XStream.setupDefaultSecurity()`. Deserializing untrusted XML with XStream without strict type controls is equivalent to Java native deserialization of untrusted data.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream deserialization without type restrictions - AnyTypePermission
 * @description XStream configured with AnyTypePermission allows deserialization of any type, enabling gadget chain attacks
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-any-type-permission
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("addPermission") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream", "XStream") and
  mc.getAnArgument().(FieldAccess).getField().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.security", "AnyTypePermission")
select mc, "XStream is configured with AnyTypePermission, which allows deserialization of any type."
findings.hypothesis: XStream configured with AnyTypePermission which allows all types to be deserialized

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream Class.forName dynamic class loading in converters
 * @description Dynamic class loading from XML-controlled input in XStream converters can lead to code injection
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-dynamic-class-loading
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream converters driven by XML content

message.text: Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: XStream.registerConverterDynamically
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: RecordConverter.<clinit>
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: RecordConverter.classForName
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: .resolveClass
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.<clinit>
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.loadClassForName
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.getStaxInputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.getStaxInputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.getStaxOutputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: JVM.getStaxOutputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: CustomObjectInputStream.resolveClass
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: SjsxpDriver.createInputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: SjsxpDriver.createOutputFactory
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: XppReader.createParser
Dynamic class loading via Class.forName() in XStream code: DefaultMapper.realClass

findings.hypothesis: XStream.fromXML() called with remote/user-controlled input, enabling unsafe deserialization

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream DefaultMapper loads class from XML-controlled input
 * @description DefaultMapper.realClass() calls Class.forName() with a class name that originates from parsed XML, enabling arbitrary class instantiation
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-defaultmapper-class-loading
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

// Find where DefaultMapper.realClass is called and what feeds its argument
from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  m.hasName("realClass") and
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.mapper", "DefaultMapper") and
  mc.getMethod() = m
select mc, "DefaultMapper.realClass() dynamically loads a class by name from deserialized XML content - enables arbitrary class instantiation (CVE-2021-21351 pattern)."
findings.hypothesis: DefaultMapper.realClass() uses Class.forName() with XML-controlled class names

message.text: DefaultMapper.realClass() dynamically loads a class by name from deserialized XML content - enables arbitrary class instantiation (CVE-2021-21351 pattern).

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream DynamicProxyConverter creates proxies from XML-controlled interface names
 * @description DynamicProxyConverter uses Proxy.newProxyInstance() with interface classes loaded from XML, enabling code injection
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-dynamic-proxy-converter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("newProxyInstance") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang.reflect", "Proxy") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "DynamicProxyConverter calls Proxy.newProxyInstance() with interface types loaded from XML content - attackers can specify arbitrary handler classes."
findings.hypothesis: DynamicProxyConverter uses Proxy.newProxyInstance() with XML-controlled interface names and handler classes

message.text: DynamicProxyConverter calls Proxy.newProxyInstance() with interface types loaded from XML content - attackers can specify arbitrary handler classes.
DynamicProxyConverter calls Proxy.newProxyInstance() with interface types loaded from XML content - attackers can specify arbitrary handler classes.
DynamicProxyConverter calls Proxy.newProxyInstance() with interface types loaded from XML content - attackers can specify arbitrary handler classes.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream JavaClassConverter loads classes by name from XML
 * @description JavaClassConverter converts XML text directly to Class objects using class name lookup
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-java-class-converter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.extended", "JavaClassConverter") and
  m.hasName("fromString") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadClass")
  )
select mc, "JavaClassConverter.fromString() loads a class by name from XML content at " + mc.getLocation().toString()
findings.hypothesis: JavaClassConverter.fromString() converts XML text to Class objects, allowing arbitrary class instantiation

message.text: JavaClassConverter.fromString() loads a class by name from XML content at file:///home/USER_ANONYMIZED/github/USER_ANONYMIZED/phd/penetraitor/packages/x-stream__xstream_1.4.15/xstream/src/java/com/thoughtworks/xstream/converters/extended/JavaClassConverter.java:76:20:76:40

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream SerializableConverter uses ObjectInputStream for deserialization
 * @description SerializableConverter delegates to ObjectInputStream.readObject() which can execute gadget chains
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-serializable-converter-ois
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  (mc.getMethod().hasName("readObject") or mc.getMethod().hasName("readUnshared")) and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "ObjectInputStream") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - potential deserialization gadget chain execution."
findings.hypothesis: XStream SerializableConverter uses ObjectInputStream.readObject() to deserialize objects, enabling gadget chain attacks

message.text: XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in XStreamer.fromXML - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.
XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in XStreamer.fromXML - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.
XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in PureJavaReflectionProvider.instantiateUsingSerialization - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.
XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in CustomObjectInputStream.readUnshared - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.
XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in AnnotationMapper.readObject - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.
XStream calls ObjectInputStream.readObject() in PackageAliasingMapper.readObject - potential deserialization gadget chain execution.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream SecurityMapper type permission bypass - weak class name check
 * @description SecurityMapper may use prefix-based class name matching that can be bypassed
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-security-mapper-bypass
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

// Check WildcardTypePermission and RegExpTypePermission for weak matching patterns
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("allows") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName() = "com.thoughtworks.xstream.security" and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.mapper", "SecurityMapper")
select mc, "SecurityMapper.realClass() delegates type permission check to " + mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + ".allows() - verify this cannot be bypassed."
findings.hypothesis: SecurityMapper type permission checking can be bypassed with crafted class names

message.text: SecurityMapper.realClass() delegates type permission check to TypePermission.allows() - verify this cannot be bypassed.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream JavaMethodConverter - arbitrary method invocation from XML
 * @description JavaMethodConverter can deserialize Method objects from XML, enabling reflection-based code execution
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-java-method-converter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.extended", "JavaMethodConverter") and
  m.hasName("unmarshal") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getMethod") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getDeclaredMethod") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getConstructor") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getDeclaredConstructor") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("invoke") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass")
  )
select mc, "JavaMethodConverter.unmarshal() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() - XML content controls which Java Method/Constructor object is created, enabling arbitrary reflection."
findings.hypothesis: JavaMethodConverter can deserialize arbitrary Method/Constructor objects from XML enabling reflection-based attacks

message.text: JavaMethodConverter.unmarshal() calls getDeclaredMethod() - XML content controls which Java Method/Constructor object is created, enabling arbitrary reflection.
JavaMethodConverter.unmarshal() calls getDeclaredConstructor() - XML content controls which Java Method/Constructor object is created, enabling arbitrary reflection.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream ExternalizableConverter calls readExternal on XML-controlled objects
 * @description ExternalizableConverter calls readExternal() on deserialized objects, which can execute arbitrary code
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-externalizable-converter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("readExternal") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.reflection", "ExternalizableConverter")
select mc, "ExternalizableConverter calls readExternal() on an object whose type is controlled by XML input - this can trigger arbitrary code execution during deserialization."
findings.hypothesis: ExternalizableConverter calls readExternal() on XML-controlled object types, enabling code execution

message.text: ExternalizableConverter calls readExternal() on an object whose type is controlled by XML input - this can trigger arbitrary code execution during deserialization.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream converters performing JNDI lookups from XML content
 * @description JNDI lookups with XML-controlled names can trigger remote class loading
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-jndi-lookup
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("lookup") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("javax.naming", "Context") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "XStream code calls JNDI lookup() in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + " - XML-controlled JNDI names enable remote class loading."
findings.hypothesis: XStream converters perform JNDI lookups with XML-controlled names enabling remote class loading

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream SubjectConverter and security-sensitive converters that instantiate classes from XML
 * @description Various XStream converters instantiate classes based on XML-supplied class names
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-converter-class-instantiation
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("newInstance") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Class") or
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang.reflect", "Constructor")
  ) and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().hasName("unmarshal")
select mc, "XStream converter " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + ".unmarshal() calls newInstance() to create objects from XML-controlled class names."
findings.hypothesis: XStream converters' unmarshal methods instantiate classes from XML-controlled names via newInstance()

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream TreeUnmarshaller - class loading from XML type attribute
 * @description TreeUnmarshaller.start() loads classes from XML class attribute, which is the core deserialization entry point
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-tree-unmarshaller-class-loading
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc, Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.core", "TreeUnmarshaller") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType() = m.getDeclaringType() and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("lookupType")
  )
select mc, "TreeUnmarshaller calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() to resolve class from XML attribute - the class name comes from untrusted XML input."
findings.hypothesis: TreeUnmarshaller loads classes from XML type attributes, enabling deserialization of attacker-controlled types

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream SubjectConverter instantiates Principal classes from XML
 * @description SubjectConverter.unmarshal() reads class names from XML to instantiate Principal/Subject objects
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-subject-converter-class-load
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from Method m, MethodCall mc
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.extended", "SubjectConverter") and
  m.hasName("unmarshal") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select mc, "SubjectConverter.unmarshal() - " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() called at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " with XML-controlled data (CVE-2021-21347 pattern)."
findings.hypothesis: SubjectConverter.unmarshal() instantiates classes from XML-supplied names (CVE-2021-21347)

message.text: SubjectConverter.unmarshal() - unmarshalPrincipals() called at line 84 with XML-controlled data (CVE-2021-21347 pattern).
SubjectConverter.unmarshal() - unmarshalPublicCredentials() called at line 85 with XML-controlled data (CVE-2021-21347 pattern).
SubjectConverter.unmarshal() - unmarshalPrivateCredentials() called at line 86 with XML-controlled data (CVE-2021-21347 pattern).
SubjectConverter.unmarshal() - unmarshalReadOnly() called at line 87 with XML-controlled data (CVE-2021-21347 pattern).

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream AbstractReflectionConverter - class loading chain from XML
 * @description AbstractReflectionConverter resolves class types from XML attributes enabling type confusion attacks
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-reflection-converter-class-load
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.reflection", "AbstractReflectionConverter") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("newInstance")
  )
select mc, "AbstractReflectionConverter." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() with XML-controlled class name - enables arbitrary object deserialization."
findings.hypothesis: AbstractReflectionConverter loads and instantiates XML-controlled class types

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream URLClassLoader - remote class loading via XML-controlled URL
 * @description XStream code uses URLClassLoader with URLs that may be XML-controlled, enabling remote code execution
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-urlclassloader
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from ClassInstanceExpr cie
where
  cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("java.net", "URLClassLoader") and
  cie.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select cie, "XStream creates URLClassLoader in " + cie.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + cie.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() - if URLs come from XML, remote class loading is possible."
findings.hypothesis: XStream creates URLClassLoader with XML-controlled URLs enabling remote class loading

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream ProcessBuilder or Runtime.exec reachable from deserialization
 * @description Code execution via ProcessBuilder or Runtime.exec potentially reachable through XStream deserialization gadget chains
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-process-exec
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("exec") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Runtime")
  ) and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "Runtime.exec() call found in XStream code: " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() - potential RCE gadget."
findings.hypothesis: Runtime.exec reachable through XStream deserialization gadget chains

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream WildcardTypePermission - potential bypass with crafted class names
 * @description WildcardTypePermission uses simple glob matching which may be bypassed with crafted package/class names
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-wildcard-permission-bypass
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

// Find string operations in WildcardTypePermission that could be bypassed
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.security", "WildcardTypePermission") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().hasName("allows") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("matches") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("endsWith") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("contains") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("getName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("replace")
  )
select mc, "WildcardTypePermission.allows() uses " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() for class name matching at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - verify matching logic is not bypassable with crafted class names."
findings.hypothesis: WildcardTypePermission glob matching can be bypassed with crafted class names to bypass security restrictions

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream CGLIBEnhancedConverter - class loading and proxy creation from XML
 * @description CGLIBEnhancedConverter creates CGLIB proxies from XML-controlled class names, enabling code injection
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-cglib-converter
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.reflection", "CGLIBEnhancedConverter") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().hasName("unmarshal") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("newInstance") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("create") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("setSuperclass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("setInterfaces") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("setCallback")
  )
select mc, "CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection."
findings.hypothesis: CGLIBEnhancedConverter creates CGLIB proxies from XML-controlled class names (CVE-2021-21344/21345 pattern)

message.text: CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls setSuperclass() at line 352 - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection.
CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls realClass() at line 358 - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection.
CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls setInterfaces() at line 361 - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection.
CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls create() at line 397 - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection.
CGLIBEnhancedConverter.unmarshal() calls create() at line 403 - CGLIB proxy creation from XML-controlled class names enables code injection.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream - javax.xml.transform EventHandler gadget chain reachability
 * @description EventHandler or similar gadget-supporting classes instantiated within XStream deserialization paths
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-eventhandler-gadget
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

// Look for invocations from within XStream converters that use reflection Method.invoke()
// which could be part of a gadget chain triggered by EventHandler
from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("invoke") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang.reflect", "Method") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("com.thoughtworks.xstream%")
select mc, "Method.invoke() called in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context."
findings.hypothesis: Method.invoke() calls within XStream deserialization context enabling gadget chain execution via reflection

message.text: Method.invoke() called in BeanProperty.get() at line 99 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in BeanProperty.set() at line 118 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in BeanProvider.visitSerializableProperties() at line 94 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in BeanProvider.writeProperty() at line 116 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in AbstractAttributedCharacterIteratorAttributeConverter.getName() at line 67 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in CGLIBEnhancedConverter.createCallbackIndexMap() at line 264 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in PureJavaReflectionProvider.instantiateUsingSerialization() at line 108 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in JVM.<clinit>() at line 104 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in Cloneables.clone() at line 45 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in SerializationMembers.callReadResolve() at line 74 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in SerializationMembers.callWriteReplace() at line 97 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in SerializationMembers.callReadObject() at line 126 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.
Method.invoke() called in SerializationMembers.callWriteObject() at line 146 - reflection invocation from XStream deserialization context.

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream SerializationMembers invokes readResolve/readObject via reflection - gadget chain trigger
 * @description SerializationMembers calls readResolve(), readObject(), and writeReplace() via Method.invoke() on 
 *              XML-controlled objects, directly enabling deserialization gadget chain execution
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-serialization-members-gadget-trigger
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 *       external/cwe/cwe-094
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.core.util", "SerializationMembers") and
  mc.getMethod().hasName("invoke") and
  mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang.reflect", "Method")
select mc, "SerializationMembers." + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "() calls Method.invoke() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - this reflectively invokes readResolve/readObject/writeReplace on XML-controlled objects, enabling gadget chain execution (CWE-502/CWE-94)."
findings.hypothesis: SerializationMembers.callReadResolve/callReadObject invoke arbitrary methods on XML-controlled objects via reflection

message.text: SerializationMembers.callReadResolve() calls Method.invoke() at line 74 - this reflectively invokes readResolve/readObject/writeReplace on XML-controlled objects, enabling gadget chain execution (CWE-502/CWE-94).

SerializationMembers.callWriteReplace() calls Method.invoke() at line 97 - this reflectively invokes readResolve/readObject/writeReplace on XML-controlled objects, enabling gadget chain execution (CWE-502/CWE-94).

SerializationMembers.callReadObject() calls Method.invoke() at line 126 - this reflectively invokes readResolve/readObject/writeReplace on XML-controlled objects, enabling gadget chain execution (CWE-502/CWE-94).

SerializationMembers.callWriteObject() calls Method.invoke() at line 146 - this reflectively invokes readResolve/readObject/writeReplace on XML-controlled objects, enabling gadget chain execution (CWE-502/CWE-94).

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream missing security setup - no type allowlist configured
 * @description XStream instances created without calling setupDefaultSecurity() or addPermission() are vulnerable
 *              to deserialization of arbitrary types
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-missing-security-setup
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

// Find XStream instantiation sites that don't subsequently call security setup methods
from ClassInstanceExpr cie
where
  cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream", "XStream") and
  not exists(MethodCall mc |
    mc.getQualifier() = cie or
    (
      mc.getMethod().hasName("setupDefaultSecurity") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("addPermission") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("allowTypes") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("allowTypesByWildcard") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("allowTypeHierarchy") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("denyTypes") or
      mc.getMethod().hasName("denyTypesByWildcard")
    )
  )
select cie, "XStream instance created at " + cie.getLocation().toString() + " - verify that security permissions (setupDefaultSecurity/addPermission/allowTypes) are configured before deserializing untrusted input."
findings.hypothesis: XStream instances created without security setup are vulnerable to deserialization of arbitrary types

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name XStream EnumConverter and EnumSetConverter - class loading from XML
 * @description Enum converters load enum classes by name from XML content, enabling arbitrary class loading
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/xstream-enum-converter-class-load
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-470
 *       external/cwe/cwe-502
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.enums", "EnumConverter") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.enums", "EnumSetConverter") or
    mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("com.thoughtworks.xstream.converters.enums", "EnumMapConverter")
  ) and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().hasName("unmarshal") and
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("realClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("forName") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("loadClass") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("valueOf") or
    mc.getMethod().hasName("newInstance")
  )
select mc, mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getName() + ".unmarshal() calls " + mc.getMethod().getName() + "() at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine() + " - enum class loaded from XML-controlled name enables arbitrary class loading."
findings.hypothesis: Enum converters load enum classes by name from XML enabling arbitrary class loading

message.text: EnumConverter.unmarshal() calls valueOf() at line 55 - enum class loaded from XML-controlled name enables arbitrary class loading.
EnumMapConverter.unmarshal() calls realClass() at line 65 - enum class loaded from XML-controlled name enables arbitrary class loading.
EnumSetConverter.unmarshal() calls realClass() at line 83 - enum class loaded from XML-controlled name enables arbitrary class loading.
